title: Chapter 2060: Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence
content:
Chapter 2060: Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence

"Of course. Like I said, what’s mine is yours," Feng Yuxiang said with a calm nod.

She continued, "To be honest with you, Young Master, I’ve always been a little envious that you possess so much dragon power but almost no phoenix power. It makes my bloodline feel a little inferior. Hopefully, this will help balance things out."

"It’s not like I am purposefully picking dragon powers over phoenix powers. It’s just that I’ve had more encounters with dragons than phoenixes," Yuan said. "In any case, I’ll accept it. Thank you."

Feng Yuxiang handed the Primordial Phoenix’s Blood Essence to him the next moment. This then reminded Yuan that he still had the Dragon Ancestor’s Blood Essence.

"I will need a quiet and secure place to absorb this blood essence, especially since I don’t know how long it’d take..."

At first, he considered absorbing the blood essence in Senior Bai’s world within the Myriad of Techniques, since he was familiar with the place and it was accessible to only a select few. However, there was a chance that his cultivation might become violent, and he didn’t want to risk causing damage to the place.

Eventually, Yuan decided to find a secluded place somewhere in the Eighth Heaven to consume the blood essence. However, he didn’t know where to go.

This was when Feng Yuxiang spoke up again, "Young Master, if you’re looking for a place to enter seclusion, why don’t you visit my Immortal Cave? It’s located in the Eighth Heaven."

"Oh? That sounds like a good idea. Where is it?"

"I’ll take you there."

She retrieved her flying treasure and boarded it a moment later. Once Yuan was aboard, she guided it into motion.

"It might take some time to get there since it’s quite far away," she said.

Yuan nodded, and in the meantime, studied the Mystic Sword Tablet.

Several months later, they arrived at a place of searing heat, where volcanoes and rivers of flowing magma stretched in every direction, as if an apocalypse had recently swept through the land.

This sort of extreme environment would’ve been great for Yuan to train his fire resistance before, but he was already at the point where the heat didn’t affect him in the slightest.

Feng Yuxiang finally brought the flying treasure to a halt and said, "Young Master, we’ve arrived at the location, but there’s a problem..."

Yuan stowed away the Mystic Sword Tablet and stepped to the edge of the flying treasure to see for himself.

In the distance, he spotted several groups of cultivators encircling a particular area. Judging by their distinct robes, each group appeared to hail from a different faction.

"Is that where your Immortal Cave is located?" Yuan asked Feng Yuxiang.

However, Feng Yuxiang shook her head and said, "No, my Immortal Cave is a little further ahead. However, with so many people here, I don’t think it’ll be a good idea to use it anymore."

Yuan then said, "There’s no need to come to a conclusion so quickly. Let’s check out the situation first before we decide what to do next."

Yuan leapt from the flying treasure and flew toward these cultivators, who were all within the Immortal Realm.

There were about seven hundred cultivators gathered there, most of them ranging from Bronze Immortal to Silver Immortal. Within each group, a single Golden Immortal stood at the forefront, acting as their leader.

When these cultivators noticed Yuan, they immediately became alert.

"Stop right there." One of the Golden Immortals demanded in a tone of authority.

Yuan slowly came to a halt, but before he could even open his mouth, the Golden Immortal continued, "Turn around and leave immediately. We’re busy."

Because Yuan’s cultivation was only at the Bronze Immortal stage, the Golden Immortal didn’t even bother to show him any courtesy and brazenly ordered him to leave.

"That would be difficult, as I have some business ahead," Yuan said.

"That business will have to wait. If you know what’s good for you, leave while I’m still patient enough to let you!" the Golden Immortal snapped, his voice rising sharply.

"This bastard... Who the hell do you think you are to speak to my Young Master in that tone? Have you lost your damn mind?!" Feng Yuxiang snapped as she caught up with Yuan.

Instantly, all eyes shifted to her—a peerless beauty with flaming red hair and golden eyes, unmistakably marking her as a beast in human form. Yet what truly seized their attention was the terrifying aura of her True Immortal cultivation.

Over the past several years, her cultivation had recovered enough to surpass Yuan once more, now just a hair’s breadth away from stepping into the God Ascension Realm.

The Golden Immortal who commanded Yuan tried to respond but was quickly interrupted, "First of all, you’re all in my territory. If anyone should leave, it’s you lot."

"Your territory...? I’m sorry, but we’ve traveled through this region for the past hundred years, and this is the first time we’ve ever seen you. Who exactly are you people?" another Golden Immortal asked, his tone far more respectful now that they were standing before a True Immortal—someone who could easily kill them all.

"A mere hundred years?" Feng Yuxiang scoffed with a cold sneer. "To an Immortal, a hundred years could easily pass in the blink of an eye. You might as well admit you’ve only just arrived with such a trivial span of time."

What Feng Yuxiang said made sense, but it was not like they could just leave, especially after pouring so many resources into their business.

"Ah, it seems we’ve forgotten to introduce ourselves. I am the Second Elder of the Blazing Orchid Sect, and—" The Golden Immortal tried to reveal his background, but Feng Yuxiang couldn’t care less and said.

"I don’t give a hot damn about your identity or where you came from. If you don’t apologize to my Young Master and get lost within ten seconds, I’ll turn you all into ashes."

Feng Yuxiang threatened as she revealed some of her flames and unique aura.

"A phoenix!" These Immortals quickly realized her identity and swallowed nervously.
